authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Lindsey Lake Upper not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Lindsey Lake Upper not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW
sources.quotedText: R2 Nevada Lindsey Lake Upper Rainbow Trout Fingerling Put and Grow May-September

sources.title: Pre-Stocking Evaluation at Upper Lindsey Lake, Nevada County — CDFW North Central Region (Nov. 9, 2020)
sources.quotedText: Upper Lindsey Lake (Lake ID: 12654) is located in Nevada County north of Emigrant Gap on Interstate 80 at an elevation of 6,438 feet above sea level (Figures 1 & 2). The lake is accessed by foot via the Lindsey Lake Trailhead at Lindsey Lake Campground. The lake is managed by Pacific Gas & Electric Company (PG&E) as part of the Drum-Spaulding Hydroelectric Project (FERC# 2310). The California Department of Fish and Wildlife (CDFW) has planted Upper Lindsey Lake since at least 1950. CDFW most recently planted the lake in 2018 with fingerling Rainbow Trout (Oncorhynchus mykiss; RT).

sources.title: CDFW California Lakes GIS layer — record for DFGWATERID 12654 ("Lindsey Lakes"/Upper Lindsey Lake), Nevada County, CA, cross-referenced to GNIS ID 266913
sources.quotedText: {"DFGWATERID":12654,"NAME":"Lindsey Lakes","GNIS_NAME":"Lindsey Lakes","GNIS_ID":266913,"COUNTY":"Nevada","LAT_NAD83":39.41447102,"LON_NAD83":-120.63101457,"elev_ft":6438}; elevation of 6,438 ft matches the CDFW pre-stocking evaluation's stated elevation for Upper Lindsey Lake (Lake ID 12654), and the coordinate (-120.63101, 39.41447) matches the file's recorded coordinate (-120.6311, 39.4145) to within ~10m. The underlying GNIS ID 266913 ("Lindsey Lakes") is confirmed independently via OpenStreetMap's gnis:feature_id tag on the same water body (source: NHD); it does not appear in the carto.nationalmap.gov GNIS ArcGIS mirror's California hydrographic-features layer, which is missing this minor-lake record entirely.

parameters.note: Lindsey Lake Upper (Upper Lindsey Lake) is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Lindsey Lake Upper (Region 2, Nevada Co.) for fingerling-size Rainbow Trout under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of May-September. CDFW's 2020 pre-stocking evaluation independently documents this lake's long history of aerial fingerling Rainbow Trout plants (typically ~4,000 fish). This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
